source: KMP fake-transport service-readiness and virtual-clock policy vectors distilled into a pre-migration readiness manifest
description: Pins the base fake-transport controls that must remain executable before deterministic runtime orchestration delegates to shared KMP code.

expected.commonDecision: Fake-transport base runtime migration may proceed only after scripted command outcomes, delayed-response polling, reconnect-after-failure controls, retry-delay scheduling, unscripted-operation timeouts, service readiness, and virtual-clock timeout policy vectors remain executable from shared commonTest, Android and iOS guard the same vector paths, platform facade compatibility stays explicit, and the shared tests are compile-verified.

commonRuntimePrototype.status: executable shared commonTest fake-transport readiness guard
executable shared commonTest fake-transport readiness guard

notes: This readiness manifest covers base fake-transport controls only. Feature-specific runtime slices still need Android facade tests, iOS facade tests, shared common fake-transport tests, and public error/cancellation/timeout mapping before production delegation.
